destinations.evidence: Scheduled async commitment construction passes the scheduled caller as original async sender.
Settlement uses TxScheduled.caller for scheduled originals and writes commitment_tx/settlement_tx metadata into Scheduled payloads.
destinations.snippet: OriginalTransaction::Scheduled(tx) => tx.tx().caller ... TransactionSigned::Scheduled(signed) => { tx.commitment_tx = Some(commitment_hash); tx.settlement_tx = Some(settlement_hash); tx.spc_calls = Some(spc_calls.clone()); }

destinations.evidence: Scheduled payload carries caller plus commitment_tx/settlement_tx/spc_calls fields.
destinations.snippet: pub caller: Address; pub commitment_tx: Option<B256>; pub settlement_tx: Option<B256>; pub spc_calls: Option<Vec<SpcCall>>

rationale: The settlement and metadata candidates preserve caller/metadata fields needed by downstream async accounting.
statement: When payload construction handles scheduled async work, settlement calldata must charge the scheduled transaction's original caller rather than the transaction signer, and scheduled transaction payloads must carry commitment_tx and settlement_tx metadata when those async pool and settlement values are present.
suggested_falsification_tests: Scheduled async tx with signer SYSTEM_SENDER and caller user should charge caller.
Fulfilled scheduled async should carry commitment_tx and settlement_tx.
summary: Scheduled async caller context and metadata propagation are mapped.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Scheduled async settlement fixture with different signer/caller should charge caller and preserve metadata fields.
title: Scheduled async metadata and settlement use scheduled caller context
